title: Blechnum areolare
taxonomy: Blechnum areolare is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It belongs to the genus Blechnum, which includes over 100 species of terrestrial and epiphytic ferns. The family Blechnaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as the chain ferns. This classification places Blechnum areolare within the larger group of vascular plants and more specifically, the pteridophytes. The plant is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas, Africa, and Australia.

Its extracts are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating skin conditions and wounds. intro: Blechnum plicatum, also known as Blechnum areolare, is a species of fern native to New Zealand and Australia. Traditionally, Blechnum areolare has been used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which provide health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Blechnum areolare include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
